title: Pyrus royleana
taxonomy: Pyrus royleana is classified under the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. The genus Pyrus is a group of flowering plants in the family Rosaceae, consisting of several species of wild and cultivated pears. Pyrus royleana is a specific species within the genus Pyrus and is native to the Himalayan region. The species is also known as the Royle's pear, and is characterized by its unique characteristics such as its fruit and leaf morphology. It is closely related to other species within the genus Pyrus, which are also commonly known as pears.
uses: Pyrus royleana, commonly known as the Royle's apple, is a species of apple tree native to the Himalayan region. parts: Pyrus royleana, also known as the Himalayan wild apple, is a plant species found in the Himalayas. intro: Pyrus royleana, also known as the Royle's wild apple, is a deciduous tree species native to the Himalayan region. It has been utilized in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus royleana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus royleana include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
